checkpoints_by_week.week-01: Can explain what each main computer component does and how a system boots.
checkpoints_by_week.week-02: Can identify common devices, ports, and SOHO troubleshooting steps.
checkpoints_by_week.week-03: Can compare major operating systems and explain basic installation choices.
checkpoints_by_week.week-04: Can follow a support process and document incidents clearly.
checkpoints_by_week.week-05: Can navigate Linux comfortably enough to start labs and tooling.
checkpoints_by_week.week-06: Can describe how data moves through a simple network.
checkpoints_by_week.week-07: Can explain when a switch is enough and when a router is required.
checkpoints_by_week.week-08: Can read and plan IPv4 subnets for small environments.
checkpoints_by_week.week-09: Can narrate the service path from 'join network' to 'page loads.'
checkpoints_by_week.week-10: Can explain baseline protections for a small office or home network.
checkpoints_by_week.week-11: Can use packet captures to support an explanation instead of guessing.
checkpoints_by_week.week-12: Can explain internet plumbing clearly enough for a manager.
checkpoints_by_week.week-13: Can define cybersecurity in business terms, not just technical terms.
checkpoints_by_week.week-14: Can distinguish risk from vulnerability and link controls to business priorities.
checkpoints_by_week.week-15: Can describe how network design choices affect security outcomes.
checkpoints_by_week.week-16: Can navigate Linux and explain permissions without memorizing blindly.
checkpoints_by_week.week-17: Can read and write basic SQL queries for security-relevant data.
checkpoints_by_week.week-18: Can classify assets and explain why not all data deserves the same treatment.
checkpoints_by_week.week-19: Can reason from asset to attacker to mitigation.
checkpoints_by_week.week-20: Can outline the incident lifecycle and the evidence sources used along the way.
checkpoints_by_week.week-21: Can explain how a SOC turns alerts into actions and communications.
checkpoints_by_week.week-22: Can read and modify short Python scripts without freezing.
checkpoints_by_week.week-23: Can connect automation ideas to real analyst workflows.
checkpoints_by_week.week-24: Can talk about your work in evidence-based, employer-friendly language.
checkpoints_by_week.week-25: Your online profile and job-search system are live and usable.
checkpoints_by_week.week-26: Can explain core defensive principles clearly and consistently.
checkpoints_by_week.week-27: Can translate technical incidents into management language.
checkpoints_by_week.week-28: Can think about incidents in terms of preparation, roles, and business continuity.
checkpoints_by_week.week-29: Can talk about cyber in governance and resilience terms, not just tools.
checkpoints_by_week.week-30: Can describe a defensive workflow from alert to escalation.
checkpoints_by_week.week-31: You have a usable portfolio and a credible next-step roadmap.
checkpoints_by_week.week-32: Everything is either complete or deliberately parked with a next step.

workload_model.added_practice_output_hours: Why the schedule is longer than a simple 15h/week divide
Core study timeline stays at 32 weeks if you use the review day for light website upkeep. If you treat the website as a separate project, budget about 1-2 extra weeks.

seed_rows.Root cause: Weak MFA, exposed service, poor patching, bad monitoring, etc.
seed_rows.Management summary: Write a manager-ready summary of business impact and response.
seed_rows.What control would I change?: Name the preventive or detective control you would strengthen.
seed_rows.Owner / stakeholder: CISO, IT Ops, Vendor, HR, Legal...

overview_lines: Cybersecurity Roadmap Study Plan (32-week core plan + portfolio companion)
Built for 2.5 hours per day, 5 study days per week, 1 review day, and 1 rest day. This version adds filterable task tags, a website companion map, and portfolio-aware study cues.
Study hours per active day | 2.5 | Planned content hours | 376.1
New-content days per week | 5 | Core weeks to finish content | 31
Review days per week | 1 | Recommended buffer weeks | 1
Rest days per week | 1 | Recommended total calendar weeks | 32
New-content hours per week | 12.5 | Approximate calendar months | 7.4
Total weekly scheduled hours | 15 | Completion note | 31 active content weeks + 1 buffer / catch-up week
Website companion assumption | Build once, then update lightly on review day
Separate website-only timeline | Add ~1-2 weeks if you do site work outside study hours
